title: Uragoga subspathacea
intro: Uragoga subspathacea, also known as the Jamaican dogwood, is a plant species native to the Caribbean region. The Jamaican dogwood is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to alleviate pain and anxiety due to its sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga subspathacea include alkaloids, particularly theobromine and theophylline, as well as flavonoids and other compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Jamaican dogwood include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to treat insomnia, restlessness, and other conditions associated with stress and anxiety.
